channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 Restrain and Hedge: A New U.S. Nuclear Strategy for a Two-Peer World | War on the Rocks
item.description: What if fielding more nuclear weapons makes the United States less secure, not more? That question is now at the center of a growing debate as the United States confronts a nuclear landscape shaped by two major nuclear rivals.China is rapidly expanding and modernizing its…

item.title: 🌏 South Korea’s 500,000 Drone Warriors Will Be a Hollow Force | War on the Rocks
item.description: Ukraine has reshaped the battlefield with cheap, expendable drones. South Korea reads the signals and wants to match the scale. North Korea has been reading the same signals through a more direct channel. Since late 2024, North Korea has rotated thousands of troops through…

item.title: 🌏 Not just commercial litigation: China is trying to keep Darwin Port | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Litigation by the Chinese lessee of Darwin Port is an attempt at stymying the Australian government’s stated aim of returning the facility to Australian control. The case started at the World Bank is no mere ...

item.title: 🛡️ What Makes Trump’s New Counterterrorism Strategy So Alarming | Foreign Policy
item.description: Most worrisome are the terrorist threats missing from the document.

item.title: 🏴 How Europe Found Its Nerve | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Trump's overreach has finally forged continental unity.

item.title: 📍 Can Corporate America Protect Democracy? | Foreign Affairs
item.description: In the Trump era, CEOs need to define redlines.

item.title: 🔐 After Mythos, Australia should prepare to battle for access to frontier AI | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The lesson of Anthropic’s new Claude Mythos AI model, with potent hacking capabilities, is becoming clear: in the era of increasingly powerful AI, national security priorities will prevail. Countries like Australia can’t assume they’ll always ...

item.title: 🌍 CENTCOM Commander Dismisses Reports That Iran Retains Most Of Its Missile And Drone Arsenal (Updated) | The War Zone
item.description: Admiral Brad Cooper said that "the numbers that I've seen in open source are not accurate" as reports mount that most of Iran's arsenal is intact. The post CENTCOM Commander Dismisses Reports That Iran Retains Most Of Its Missile And Drone Arsenal (Updated) appeared first on The…

item.title: 📍 Golden Dome defenders push back on $1.2T cost estimate | Defense One
item.description: Program leader says CBO report based on Trump’s executive order doesn’t reflect current plans.

item.title: 📍 US infantry’s drone-warfare experiments are getting bigger | Defense One
item.description: Project Flytrap 5.0 focused on working in teams of hundreds, and larger operations are on the way.

item.title: 🌏 China fires verbal warning shot at US over Taiwan | Defense News
item.description: Any misstep over Taiwan could push the U.S. and China toward direct confrontation, Chinese leader Xi Jinping told President Donald Trump.

item.title: 📍 US global leadership depends on a reliable, modern energy system | Atlantic Council
item.description: US global leadership relies on resilient, diversified energy systems—and the policy and investment foresight required to sustain this foundation. The post US global leadership depends on a reliable, modern energy system appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🛡️ Lawmakers push for domestic shipbuilding fixes as US Navy explores overseas options | Defense News
item.description: The service released its fiscal 2027 shipbuilding plan on Monday, outlining the possibility of the U.S. manufacturing ships at foreign shipyards.

item.title: 🌏 Talking (and Not Talking) Taiwan | Foreign Policy
item.description: A tale of two readouts.

item.title: 🕵️ ODNI assigns two to coordinate spy agencies on election security | Defense One
item.description: For months, it was unclear if ODNI ever named an election threats executive responsible for leading election security efforts in the 2026 midterm cycle.

item.title: 📍 To prepare for supply-chain shocks, focus on systems of disruption | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Supply chains built for efficiency are colliding with a world defined by disruption. In this context, Australia now faces a structural challenge in securing critical inputs such as fuel and fertiliser. Government, industry and consumers ...

item.title: 🏴 Putin’s parade projected weakness but he is now more dangerous than ever | Atlantic Council
item.description: While the Kremlin dictator is clearly in a weakened position, a diminished Vladimir Putin could be more dangerous than ever, write Maksym Beznosiuk and William Dixon. The post Putin’s parade projected weakness but he is now more dangerous than ever appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌏 Day 1 of Beijing Summit Produces No Big Wins for Trump | Foreign Policy
item.description: From the Iran war to trade, the U.S. president failed to secure major concessions from his counterpart.

item.title: 🌏 Trump, Xi Hold High-Stakes Summit in Beijing | Foreign Policy
item.description: From the Iran war to trade, the U.S. president failed to secure major concessions from his counterpart.

item.title: ⚛️ The Pentagon wants 10,000 small cruise missiles. Here’s who is making them. | Breaking Defense
item.description: Editor-in-Chief Aaron Mehta explains the newest Pentagon munitions push.

item.title: 🏴 Zelenskyy raises alarm over Russia’s escalating ‘human safari’ in Ukraine | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has raised the alarm over Russia’s escalating “human safari” tactics targeting the civilian population in southern Ukraine’s Kherson region, writes Oleksandr Tolokonnikov. The post Zelenskyy raises alarm over Russia’s escalating ‘human…

item.title: 📍 To deliver for the warfighter, acquisition reform must provide a level playing field | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] When all companies in the market are allowed to compete under the same set of rules, innovation flourishes and our nation unleashes the full breadth of its technological strength.

item.title: 📍 Gerald R. Ford to return from historic deployment on Saturday: CNO | Breaking Defense
item.description: Aircraft from Carrier Air Wing 8 embarked on the Ford returned to their home naval air stations on Monday.

item.title: ⚛️ Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships Will Reverse One Of The Navy’s “Largest Mistakes”: Navy Boss | The War Zone
item.description: The Chief of Naval Operations says giving up on nuclear-powered surface combatants was one of the worst decisions his service has ever made. The post Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships Will Reverse One Of The Navy’s “Largest Mistakes”: Navy Boss appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🌍 Iran military threat is diminished but not eliminated, CENTCOM chief says | Defense News
item.description: Adm. Brad Cooper said the U.S. had severely degraded Iran’s warfighting capacity, including the elimination of roughly 90% of its inventory of naval mines.

item.title: 🌍 Five ways the Iran war will forever alter the Middle East | Atlantic Council
item.description: Expect the war to weaken the Iranian regime, keep the US in the Middle East, harm Israel's standing in the region, and more. The post Five ways the Iran war will forever alter the Middle East appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Israel’s Lebanon Strategy Is Self-Defeating | Foreign Policy
item.description: A weakened state can’t disarm Hezbollah.

item.title: 🌍 In Talks with Israel, Lebanon is Walking a Thin Line | War on the Rocks
item.description: This week, Israel and Lebanon are holding their third round of unprecedented direct talks on crucial issues, including borders, the disarmament of Hizballah, and ongoing Israeli military action. The United States, which is clearly driving these talks, views them expansively as…

item.title: 🏴 Bulgaria is unlikely to become Putin’s new proxy within the European Union | Atlantic Council
item.description: Newly elected Bulgarian Prime Minister Rumen Radev is unlikely to replace recently ousted Hungarian leader Viktor Orban as Vladimir Putin's proxy within the EU and NATO, writes Kristian Kafozov. The post Bulgaria is unlikely to become Putin’s new proxy within the European Union…

item.title: 🌍 Iran’s Navy won’t rebuild for 5 to 10 years, country unable to support proxies: CENTCOM | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Today, Hamas, Hezbollah and the Houthis are all cut off from Iran’s weapons supply and support,” Adm. Brad Cooper, told SASC members.
